TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}
TEXT.source: told by Gi…ęgi…ęaw of Va…ęulu village

S.FORM: malada nu aya, maretima…ęima…ęi a tsemas niamadju.
S.PHON: ma…≠ada nu aja, mar…ôtima éima éi a ¶…ômas niama…Ču.
S.FORM: malada nu aya, maretimaljimalji a cemas niamadju.
S.PHON: ma…≠ada nu aja, mar…ôtima éima éi a ¶…ômas niama…Ču.
S.TRANSL: When we speak of shamans, they have various gods.

S.FORM: …ęakua tjara tadjaladjalan a sidjapesan a sikapalisian a paga…ęu.
S.PHON: éakua cara ta…Ča…≠a…Ča…≠an a si…Čap…ôsan a sikapa…≠isian a paga éu.
S.FORM: ljakua tjara tadjaladjalan a sidjapesan a sikapalisian a pagalju.
S.PHON: éakua ¶ara ta…Ča…≠a…Ča…≠an a si…Čap…ôsan a sikapa…≠isian a paga éu.
S.TRANSL: But they are all more or less the same in their blowing (and) rituals.

S.FORM: aitsu a malada nu izua saqetju djapesen niamadju.
S.PHON: ai ¶u a ma…≠ada nu izua saq…ôcu …Čap…ôs…ôn niama…Ču.
S.FORM: aicu a malada nu izua saqetju djapesen niamadju.
S.PHON: ai ¶u a ma…≠ada nu izua saq…ô ¶u …Čap…ôs…ôn niama…Ču.
S.TRANSL: When someone is in pain the shamans blow on them.

S.FORM: nu selapay sitjetsemas niamadju a malada, sa djau…ęan tua salasaladj niamadju, sana kapalisilisi niamadju.
S.PHON: nu s…ô…≠apaj sic…ô ¶…ômas niama…Ču a ma…≠ada, sa …Čau éan tua sa…≠asa…≠a…Č niama…Ču, sana kapa…≠isi…≠isi niama…Ču.
S.FORM: nu selapay sitjecemas niamadju a malada, sa djauljan tua salasaladj niamadju, sana kapalisilisi niamadju.
S.PHON: nu s…ô…≠apaj si ¶…ô ¶…ômas niama…Ču a ma…≠ada, sa …Čau éan tua sa…≠asa…≠a…Č niama…Ču, sana kapa…≠isi…≠isi niama…Ču.
S.TRANSL: If it‚Äôs a severe case the shamans go into a trance for them and summon their spirit companions and perform rites.

S.FORM: qau nu palisi tiamadju, venay itjen tua tjinutsauan.
S.PHON: qau nu pa…≠isi tiama…Ču, v…ônaj ic…ôn tua cinu ¶auan.
S.FORM: qau nu palisi tiamadju, venay itjen tua tjinucauan.
S.PHON: qau nu pa…≠isi tiama…Ču, v…ônaj i ¶…ôn tua ¶inu ¶auan.
S.TRANSL: When they perform rites, we give gifts for the spirits.

S.FORM: vaqu a sipatjutsautsau.
S.PHON: vaqu a sipacu ¶au ¶au.
S.FORM: vaqu a sipatjucaucau.
S.PHON: vaqu a sipa ¶u ¶au ¶au.
S.TRANSL: Millet is the gift for summoning spirits to human beings(?).

S.FORM: nu neka, paisu anga, lami anga a zuma.
S.PHON: nu n…ôka, paisu aŇča, …≠ami aŇča a zuma.
S.FORM: nu neka, paisu anga, lami anga a zuma.
S.PHON: nu n…ôka, paisu aŇča, …≠ami aŇča a zuma.
S.TRANSL: If we haven‚Äôt any, then money or some other staple food.

S.FORM: saka izua uta a sinikipapuluqem a sinitjuap a sinikipuvatju…ęayan a tja pinakitjukuda nu venay itjen.
S.PHON: saka izua uta a sinikipapu…≠uq…ôm a sinicuap a sinikipuvacu éajan a ca pinakicukuda nu v…ônaj ic…ôn.
S.FORM: saka izua uta a sinikipapuluqem a sinitjuap a sinikipuvatjuljayan a tja pinakitjukuda nu venay itjen.
S.PHON: saka izua uta a sinikipapu…≠uq…ôm a sini ¶uap a sinikipuva ¶u éajan a ¶a pinaki ¶ukuda nu v…ônaj i ¶…ôn.
S.TRANSL: And we also differentiate between gifts for success, for summoning spirits, for charms(?).

S.FORM: nu selapay aravats tja sitsu…ęu tua qatsang.
S.PHON: nu s…ô…≠apaj arava ¶ ca si ¶u éu tua qa ¶aŇč.
S.FORM: nu selapay aravac tja siculju tua qacang.
S.PHON: nu s…ô…≠apaj arava ¶ ¶a si ¶u éu tua qa ¶aŇč.
S.TRANSL: If it‚Äôs a very serious case we kill a pig.

S.FORM: nu tsemu…ęu izua za zinatjak aya a sinipatjavang tua tsemas.
S.PHON: nu ¶…ômu éu izua za zinacak aja a sinipacavaŇč tua ¶…ômas.
S.FORM: nu cemulju izua za zinatjak aya a sinipatjavang tua cemas.
S.PHON: nu ¶…ômu éu izua za zina ¶ak aja a sinipa ¶avaŇč tua ¶…ômas.
S.TRANSL: When we kill it, part is ‚Äėset aside‚Äô as an offering to the gods (to be eaten only by the immediate family).

S.FORM: azua zuma nu vutju…ęan mana ika makudanga a kanen nua mareka kaka.
S.PHON: azua zuma nu vucu éan mana ika makudaŇča a kan…ôn nua mar…ôka kaka.
S.FORM: azua zuma nu vutjuljan mana ika makudanga a kanen nua mareka kaka.
S.PHON: azua zuma nu vu ¶u éan mana ika makudaŇča a kan…ôn nua mar…ôka kaka.
S.TRANSL: The rest of the meat can be eaten by the relations.

S.FORM: aitsu a qemaza…ę tjara kitsu…ęu sakamaya.
S.PHON: ai ¶u a q…ômaza é cara ki ¶u éu sakamaja.
S.FORM: aicu a qemazalj tjara kiculju sakamaya.
S.PHON: ai ¶u a q…ômaza é ¶ara ki ¶u éu sakamaja.
S.TRANSL: When there is a haunting(?) we certainly join in a sacrificial feast.

S.FORM: azua malada nu izua a naminvalak, sualapan tua mareka qatsi…ęay tua mareka valiseqa…ę tua mareka qidung.
S.PHON: azua ma…≠ada nu izua a naminva…≠ak, sua…≠apan tua mar…ôka qa ¶i éaj tua mar…ôka va…≠is…ôqa é tua mar…ôka qiduŇč.
S.FORM: azua malada nu izua a naminvalak, sualapan tua mareka qaciljay tua mareka valiseqalj tua mareka qidung.
S.PHON: azua ma…≠ada nu izua a naminva…≠ak, sua…≠apan tua mar…ôka qa ¶i éaj tua mar…ôka va…≠is…ôqa é tua mar…ôka qiduŇč.
S.TRANSL: When someone has a swelling the shaman removes it with stones, wood-chips or charcoal.

S.FORM: nu izua piqay, nu naminvalak a tja kinatsavatsavan, malaing anga a nasema varung tjayamadju tua siqaqivu.
S.PHON: nu izua piqaj, nu naminva…≠ak a ca kina ¶ava ¶avan, ma…≠aiŇč aŇča a nas…ôma varuŇč cajama…Ču tua siqaqivu.
S.FORM: nu izua piqay, nu naminvalak a tja kinacavacavan, malaing anga a nasema varung tjayamadju tua siqaqivu.
S.PHON: nu izua piqaj, nu naminva…≠ak a ¶a kina ¶ava ¶avan, ma…≠aiŇč aŇča a nas…ôma varuŇč ¶ajama…Ču tua siqaqivu.
S.TRANSL: If we have sores or swellings on our bodies, the shamans say whatever comes to their minds to explain it.

S.FORM: vinu…ęuq aya.
S.PHON: vinu éuq aja.
S.FORM: vinuljuq aya.
S.PHON: vinu éuq aja.
S.TRANSL: We are told that spirits have thrown something at us,

S.FORM: djinukul aya.
S.PHON: …Činuku…≠ aja.
S.FORM: djinukul aya.
S.PHON: …Činuku…≠ aja.
S.TRANSL: have hit us,

S.FORM: sinizurung sun aya.
S.PHON: sinizuruŇč sun aja.
S.FORM: sinizurung sun aya.
S.PHON: sinizuruŇč sun aja.
S.TRANSL: have pushed us,

S.FORM: nasetsevung sun ayain itjen.
S.PHON: nas…ô ¶…ôvuŇč sun ajain ic…ôn.
S.FORM: nasecevung sun ayain itjen.
S.PHON: nas…ô ¶…ôvuŇč sun ajain i ¶…ôn.
S.TRANSL: or we have met a spirit.

S.FORM: aitsu a malada makaya djemau…ę tua tja tjaisangas sa tja kiqaqivuan.
S.PHON: ai ¶u a ma…≠ada makaja …Č…ômau é tua ca caisaŇčas sa ca kiqaqivuan.
S.FORM: aicu a malada makaya djemaulj tua tja tjaisangas sa tja kiqaqivuan.
S.PHON: ai ¶u a ma…≠ada makaja …Č…ômau é tua ¶a ¶aisaŇčas sa ¶a kiqaqivuan.
S.TRANSL: Shamans can summon our ancestors so that we can converse with them.

S.FORM: nu izua a tja sikivadavadaq, paruqu itjen tua zaqu.
S.PHON: nu izua a ca sikivadavadaq, paruqu ic…ôn tua zaqu.
S.FORM: nu izua a tja sikivadavadaq, paruqu itjen tua zaqu.
S.PHON: nu izua a ¶a sikivadavadaq, paruqu i ¶…ôn tua zaqu.
S.TRANSL: If we have a question we put a crystal bead (on a gourd).

S.FORM: nu sitseker, …ęemaui aya.
S.PHON: nu si ¶…ôk…ôr, é…ômaui aja.
S.FORM: nu siceker, ljemaui aya.
S.PHON: nu si ¶…ôk…ôr, é…ômaui aja.
S.TRANSL: If it stays on, the answer is Yes.

S.FORM: izua i tjarivavaw a umaq nua tsemas.
S.PHON: izua i carivavaw a umaq nua ¶…ômas.
S.FORM: izua i tjarivavaw a umaq nua cemas.
S.PHON: izua i ¶arivavaw a umaq nua ¶…ômas.
S.TRANSL: The gods live in heaven.

S.FORM: nu ini anan ka patje…ęuin inika makaya a tjumaq i tjarivavaw.
S.PHON: nu ini anan ka pac…ô éuin inika makaja a cumaq i carivavaw.
S.FORM: nu ini anan ka patjeljuin inika makaya a tjumaq i tjarivavaw.
S.PHON: nu ini anan ka pa ¶…ô éuin inika makaja a ¶umaq i ¶arivavaw.
S.TRANSL: If a shaman hasn‚Äôt yet qualified she can‚Äôt get into heaven.

S.FORM: qau nu tjumaq i tjarivavaw, ventjek azua malada.
S.PHON: qau nu cumaq i carivavaw, v…ônc…ôk azua ma…≠ada.
S.FORM: qau nu tjumaq i tjarivavaw, ventjek azua malada.
S.PHON: qau nu ¶umaq i ¶arivavaw, v…ôn ¶…ôk azua ma…≠ada.
S.TRANSL: When a shaman is going to enter heaven, she performs a rite of breaking bamboo.

S.FORM: nu uri malada tiamadju mesuaw a gemdeged sana pangtjengtjezi, sana sane djaladjalan.
S.PHON: nu uri ma…≠ada tiama…Ču m…ôsuaw a g…ômd…ôg…ôd sana paŇčc…ôŇčc…ôzi, sana san…ô …Ča…≠a…Ča…≠an.
S.FORM: nu uri malada tiamadju mesuaw a gemdeged sana pangtjengtjezi, sana sane djaladjalan.
S.PHON: nu uri ma…≠ada tiama…Ču m…ôsuaw a g…ômd…ôg…ôd sana paŇč ¶…ôŇč ¶…ôzi, sana san…ô …Ča…≠a…Ča…≠an.
S.TRANSL: When shamans are going to perform a trance rite, they yawn repeatedly and then the spirits come on them, and they make their way (to heaven).

S.FORM: nu qematsuvung seman djalan uta sana patjezua.
S.PHON: nu q…ôma ¶uvuŇč s…ôman …Ča…≠an uta sana pac…ôzua.
S.FORM: nu qemacuvung seman djalan uta sana patjezua.
S.PHON: nu q…ôma ¶uvuŇč s…ôman …Ča…≠an uta sana pa ¶…ôzua.
S.TRANSL: When they have finished they make a way back and that‚Äôs the end.

S.FORM: nu makakivaqu pakasalut tua tsemas; nu makaki…ęumay uta.
S.PHON: nu makakivaqu pakasa…≠ut tua ¶…ômas; nu makaki éumaj uta.
S.FORM: nu makakivaqu pakasalut tua cemas; nu makakiljumay uta.
S.PHON: nu makakivaqu pakasa…≠ut tua ¶…ômas; nu makaki éumaj uta.
S.TRANSL: When we have finished harvesting millet we make a feast for the gods, and also when we have finished harvesting barnyard millet.

S.FORM: azua zuma nu maladan palisi a tjemamaku, palisi a keman tua paday tua masikau.
S.PHON: azua zuma nu ma…≠adan pa…≠isi a c…ômamaku, pa…≠isi a k…ôman tua padaj tua masikau.
S.FORM: azua zuma nu maladan palisi a tjemamaku, palisi a keman tua paday tua masikau.
S.PHON: azua zuma nu ma…≠adan pa…≠isi a ¶…ômamaku, pa…≠isi a k…ôman tua padaj tua masikau.
S.TRANSL: For some shamans smoking is taboo, and also eating rice and wine from the plains(?).

S.FORM: …ęakua inika palisi a zuma.
S.PHON: éakua inika pa…≠isi a zuma.
S.FORM: ljakua inika palisi a zuma.
S.PHON: éakua inika pa…≠isi a zuma.
S.TRANSL: But for some there is no such taboo.

S.FORM: nu sangasangasan vayan tua zaqu a mapu…ęat.
S.PHON: nu saŇčasaŇčasan vajan tua zaqu a mapu éat.
S.FORM: nu sangasangasan vayan tua zaqu a mapuljat.
S.PHON: nu saŇčasaŇčasan vajan tua zaqu a mapu éat.
S.TRANSL: When shamans begin they are all given a crystal bead.

S.FORM: …ęakua neka nu patsutsunan niamadju a tsemas.
S.PHON: éakua n…ôka nu pa ¶u ¶unan niama…Ču a ¶…ômas.
S.FORM: ljakua neka nu pacucunan niamadju a cemas.
S.PHON: éakua n…ôka nu pa ¶u ¶unan niama…Ču a ¶…ômas.
S.TRANSL: But they don‚Äôt really see gods.

S.FORM: …ęakua tiamadju a kinemnemnem tua kakudan niamadju.
S.PHON: éakua tiama…Ču a kin…ômn…ômn…ôm tua kakudan niama…Ču.
S.FORM: ljakua tiamadju a kinemnemnem tua kakudan niamadju.
S.PHON: éakua tiama…Ču a kin…ômn…ômn…ôm tua kakudan niama…Ču.
S.TRANSL: It‚Äôs they themselves who think up their customs.
